title: P6-MT-106-ChildProcessStallWatch-EndToEndNoSilentHangProof
group: P1 Palmistry (Tier 3 external watcher) — child-process stall watch + no-silent-hang capstone

scope.summary:
Goal: expand Palmistry (Tier 3 external watcher) from watching ONLY Handshake to ALSO watching the CHILD PROCESSES Handshake spawns (the sessions / tools / terminals / models from MT-100/101/014), so a spawned child that is ALIVE but STALLED (no progress) is detected within a BOUNDED time — and deliver THE CAPSTONE end-to-end proof that a silent, hours-long hang of an in-app operation OR a spawned child process can NEVER happen inside Handshake. This is the Palmistry half of the stall/hang detection capability; the internal_diagnostics half (the in-app StalledOperation watchdog) is MT-105, which this MT depends on and asserts against in the capstone.

MOTIVATING INCIDENT (operator, 2026-06-28): a spawned subprocess — a `cargo test` doing minidumper IPC (the MT-092 known hanger that deadlocked for HOURS) — stalled SILENTLY with no detection and no observer. The whole class (an operation OR a spawned child that hangs/stalls with no progress and no one notices) must be IMPOSSIBLE inside Handshake. MT-105 makes the in-app-operation half impossible (the internal_diagnostics watchdog); THIS MT makes the spawned-CHILD half impossible (Palmistry watches the children) AND proves both halves together in a bounded-time capstone.

WHAT PALMISTRY ALREADY DOES (reuse, do not reinvent): MT-089 = the lifecycle-inverted watcher binary that holds the PARENT (Handshake) process handle + survives parent death + records abnormal parent exit. MT-091 = freeze detection of HANDSHAKE via the DOUBLE-SIGNAL gate (stale heartbeat from the MT-084 ring + the Win32 SendMessageTimeoutW hung-window probe), with the no-false-positive-on-a-healthy-heartbeat gate, reset-on-advance, recovery, and a monotonic-Instant staleness reference (FreezeDetector/FreezeState/FreezeReport in freeze_detect.rs; the spawn_freeze_poll thread + FreezeStatus in main.rs). MT-090 = the zero-cooperation ring reader. MT-092/093 = crash capture + the durable survivor store. This MT GENERALIZES the MT-091 'watch Handshake's liveness' to 'watch each spawned CHILD's liveness' the SAME double-signal way, and reuses the MT-089 process-handle-wait + the survivor-record discipline for the children.

CHILD REGISTRATION + LIVENESS (how Palmistry learns about + watches a child): a child Handshake spawns (a terminal/model/tool/swarm session, MT-100/101/014) REGISTERS with Palmistry — over the existing MT-089 control socket (a typed `ControlMessage` variant carrying the child's pid + its liveness source) — giving Palmistry (a) the child PID (so Palmistry holds the child's OS process handle, the MT-089 OpenProcess/WaitForSingleObject pattern, to know ALIVE vs EXITED) AND (b) a per-child PROGRESS liveness signal: either the child's own ring/heartbeat slot (if the child publishes one, the MT-084 idea per-child) OR a per-child LIVENESS FILE the child touches/updates as it makes progress (an mtime/counter the watcher polls — the field-standard touch-file/keepalive technique for a process that does not share the ring). Palmistry detects a child that is ALIVE (its process handle has NOT signaled exit) but STALLED (its progress heartbeat / liveness-file has not advanced past a per-child threshold) the SAME DOUBLE-SIGNAL way MT-091 detects Handshake's freeze: stale progress signal ALONE = suspected; stale progress signal AND the corroborating signal (the child is provably alive but not progressing — process-alive + no-progress is the corroboration for a headless child that has no message pump to probe) = a CONFIRMED child stall. A child that exits cleanly, or keeps advancing its progress signal, must NEVER false-flag (the MT-091 AC-011-1 gate, per-child).

SURFACE (typed record, no free text): on a confirmed child stall, Palmistry surfaces a TYPED survivor/diagnostic record (the MT-093 survivor-record discipline / the MT-092 CrashRecord typed shape): record kind = ChildStall, the child pid, the session id, the stale duration (ms, against Palmistry's monotonic clock), the last progress counter/timestamp, and a typed reason code — NO command line, NO arguments, NO path text in the record (a local liveness-file PATH may appear only as a path REFERENCE per the MT-092/093 rule, never as project content / free text). It is logged + persisted next to the ring like the MT-089 survivor record and surfaces in the §10.12.5 Tier-3 Palmistry section of the Settings->Diagnostics panel (the same surface MT-093 feeds).

Exact files to create/edit (inside the palmistry crate + the minimal Handshake-side registration seam):
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/src/child_stall.rs` — a `ChildStallDetector` (generalize FreezeDetector: per-child last-progress tracking against a monotonic Instant, a per-child threshold, the double-signal gate process-alive + no-progress, reset-on-advance, recovery, no-false-positive) + the typed `ChildStallReport` / `ChildStallState` (Healthy / Suspected / Stalled, mirroring FreezeState). Reuse freeze_detect.rs's monotonic-reference + reset-on-advance + named-const discipline (do not duplicate the math sloppily — extract or mirror it cleanly).
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/src/child_registry.rs` — a registry of watched children: pid + a `ChildLivenessSource` (a per-child ring/heartbeat slot OR a liveness-file path) + the per-child process handle (the MT-089 ParentWatch/OpenProcess pattern, reused/generalized to a child handle: ALIVE vs EXITED). The watch loop polls each registered child's progress source + alive-state.
- EDIT `src/frontend/palmistry/src/control.rs` — add a typed `ControlMessage::RegisterChild { pid, liveness_kind, ... }` (+ a `DeregisterChild`) so Handshake tells Palmistry which children to watch (typed integers + a liveness-source reference only; no free text). Reuse the existing newline-delimited-JSON ControlMessage framing.
- EDIT `src/frontend/palmistry/src/main.rs` — spawn a CHILD-STALL poll thread (mirror spawn_freeze_poll: its OWN thread, NOT the control loop; the MT-091 ~200-500ms cadence band; reset-on-advance; debounced healthy->stalled edge), feed registered children + their progress reads into the ChildStallDetector, and on a confirmed child stall record + persist the typed ChildStall survivor record (reuse persist_survivor_record / the MT-093 store). Add the child-stall poll thread to the run_watcher teardown joins.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs` (+ backend_client.rs if needed) — the minimal Handshake-side seam: when Handshake spawns a watched child (a terminal/model/tool/swarm session from MT-100/101/014, OR — honestly — where those are typed blockers today, the supervised child processes Handshake actually does spawn), REGISTER it with Palmistry over the control socket (pid + the chosen liveness source) so the child is watched. If MT-100/101 landed typed blockers for the native spawn path (no native HTTP/IPC reach), wire the registration for the children Handshake genuinely spawns + record an HONEST typed note for the not-yet-reachable spawn paths — do NOT fabricate a watched child that Handshake does not spawn.
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/tests/test_child_stall.rs` (the per-child detector + double-signal + no-false-positive + recovery, deterministic with injected time + fake progress/alive, mirroring test_freeze_detect.rs) AND `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_no_silent_hang_end_to_end.rs` (THE CAPSTONE — see below).

THE CAPSTONE PROOF (the operator's requirement, AC-106-CAPSTONE): a single end-to-end test spawns BOTH (1) a DELIBERATELY-HUNG in-app operation (registered with the MT-105 watchdog, never ticks/completes, past its deadline) AND (2) a DELIBERATELY-HUNG real CHILD PROCESS (a spawned child that registers with Palmistry then never advances its progress signal — e.g. a tiny test-only helper binary that sleeps forever after registering, or the real palmistry test seam), and ASSERTS, BOTH within a BOUNDED time (seconds, hard-bounded): internal_diagnostics emits a `StalledOperation` (MT-105) for the hung in-app operation AND Palmistry surfaces a confirmed ChildStall (typed record) for the hung child — proving the 2026-06-28 hours-long silent hang can NEVER happen in Handshake (it is caught in seconds by BOTH tiers). The capstone uses the REAL binaries (the real HandshakeApp + the real spawned palmistry.exe + the real ring + a real hung child process) — NO mocked tier for the spec-required runtime; where a managed backend is genuinely unavailable, gate that half honestly (NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF), never fake it.

BOUNDED-TEST RULE (MT-092 precedent, MANDATORY — this is the exact failure class this MT exists to prevent): EVERY test that spawns the palmistry binary / a child process / does minidumper-IPC / waits on a child MUST use an EXPLICIT timeout (wait_for_exit / connect / read with a hard deadline) — NEVER an unbounded Command::output() or socket read_line. `#[ignore]` WITH A TYPED REASON any IPC test that deadlocks under this headless harness (run with --ignored on a real host), exactly the MT-092 precedent (minidumper out-of-process IPC + simulate_exception are the known hangers). The whole MT is about not hanging silently — its OWN tests must be hard-bounded so a deadlock cannot waste hours (the irony of the 2026-06-28 incident being a test hang is the point).

Reuse note: MT-091 freeze_detect.rs (FreezeDetector/FreezeState/FreezeReport + the monotonic-reference + reset-on-advance + double-signal + named-const discipline) is the worked reference for the per-child detector. MT-089 lifecycle.rs (ParentWatch/OpenProcess/WaitForSingleObject + SurvivorRecord + build/persist_survivor_record) is the worked reference for the per-child process handle + the typed survivor record. MT-090 ring_reader.rs is the zero-cooperation read pattern. MT-105 supplies the in-app StalledOperation the capstone asserts. The control socket + ControlMessage framing are MT-089. The §10.12.5 Tier-3 panel surface is MT-087/093. No backend; src/backend reuse-via-API only; FR kept as-is.

Risk if missed: the spawned-child half of the 2026-06-28 silent-hang class stays UNDETECTED (Palmistry watches only Handshake, not the children Handshake spawns — so a deadlocked spawned session/tool/model/test hangs forever with no observer, the exact incident); OR the capstone never proves the two tiers catch a hung operation + a hung child together in bounded time, so the operator's 'this must be IMPOSSIBLE' requirement is asserted per-tier but never end-to-end; OR (the ironic failure) a stall-detection test itself deadlocks unbounded and wastes hours, reproducing the very incident.
forbidden_paths: src/frontend/handshake_native/diag_ring/** (MT-081-owned; reuse via API — the StalledOperation code is added by MT-105, reuse it)
src/backend/** (Palmistry never touches the backend; the Handshake-side child registration is client-side only)
acceptance_criteria: AC-106-1 (CHILD STALL DETECTED — the deliverable, prove first): a test registers a child (pid + a progress liveness source), the child is ALIVE but its progress signal NEVER advances past the per-child threshold, and Palmistry declares a CONFIRMED ChildStall within a BOUNDED time and writes a typed ChildStall survivor record (kind, child pid, session id, stale_ms, last progress counter/timestamp, typed reason — no free text). A spawned child that is alive but stalled is caught in seconds, not hours.

AC-106-2 (NO FALSE POSITIVE — the per-child gate, mirrors MT-091 AC-011-1): a child that keeps advancing its progress signal faster than the threshold NEVER produces a ChildStall, across many poll ticks past the wall-clock threshold; and a child that EXITS CLEANLY (process handle signals exit) is deregistered and produces NO ChildStall (a finished child is not a stalled child). Reset-on-advance against a monotonic Instant, exactly the MT-091 discipline.

AC-106-3 (DOUBLE-SIGNAL + DEBOUNCE): a confirmed ChildStall requires BOTH the stale progress signal AND the corroboration (the child is provably ALIVE — process handle not exited — yet not progressing); a stale-but-the-child-actually-exited case is NOT a stall (it is an exit, handled separately). The ChildStall record is emitted ONCE on the healthy->stalled edge (debounced, not per poll tick). A child stall can RECOVER if the progress signal resumes (clears to Healthy, does not latch).

AC-106-4 (CHILD REGISTRATION over the control socket): the typed ControlMessage::RegisterChild { pid, liveness_kind, ... } (+ DeregisterChild) carries typed integers + a liveness-source reference only (no free text); Handshake registers a watched child over the existing MT-089 control socket. Proven by a test asserting a registered child is watched and a deregistered/exited child is dropped.

AC-106-5 (TYPED-ALLOWLIST, §6.13.8): the ChildStall survivor record + any control message carry NO project content / free text — only typed integers, enums, timestamps, and local liveness-file path references. A source + value scan asserts no command/args/free-text field on the child-stall path (the MT-093 typed-allowlist discipline, per-child).

AC-106-CAPSTONE (THE NO-SILENT-HANG PROOF — the operator's requirement): a single end-to-end test spawns BOTH a deliberately-hung in-app operation (registered with the MT-105 watchdog, never progresses, past deadline) AND a deliberately-hung real child process (registers with Palmistry, never advances its progress signal), and asserts BOTH within a BOUNDED time (seconds, hard-bounded): (a) internal_diagnostics emits a StalledOperation (MT-105) for the hung operation, AND (b) Palmistry surfaces a confirmed ChildStall (typed record) for the hung child. Run on the REAL binaries (real HandshakeApp + real spawned palmistry.exe + real ring + a real hung child) — proving the hours-long silent hang can NEVER happen in Handshake (caught in seconds by BOTH tiers).

AC-106-6 (ALL TESTS HARD-BOUNDED — MT-092 precedent, MANDATORY): every test that spawns the palmistry binary / a child process / does IPC / waits on a child uses an EXPLICIT timeout (wait_for_exit / connect / read with a hard deadline), NEVER an unbounded Command::output() / read_line; any IPC test that deadlocks headless is #[ignore]-with-a-typed-reason (run with --ignored on a real host), exactly the MT-092 minidumper-IPC precedent. A source review confirms no unbounded child wait on the stall-detection test path.

AC-106-7 (OPERATOR SURFACE): the confirmed ChildStall surfaces in the §10.12.5 Tier-3 Palmistry section of the Settings->Diagnostics panel (the MT-087/093 surface) — at minimum the record is persisted + the panel can read it (proven by the panel reading a ChildStall record, or a documented seam consistent with how MT-093 feeds the Tier-3 section).

AC-106-8: monotonic staleness reference (a process-monotonic std::time::Instant, never a wall clock) + named per-child poll-cadence/threshold consts with the relationship documented (the MT-091 AC-011-5 discipline, per-child).

AC-106-9: `cargo build -p palmistry` + `-p handshake-native` + `-p handshake-diag-ring` clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full handshake-native suite `-j 2` green; external CARGO_TARGET_DIR (palmistry-target + handshake-native-target); no repo-local target/; `git ls-files src/**/*.png` empty.
proof_targets: PT-106-A: `cargo test -p palmistry child_stall` GREEN — an alive-but-stalled child -> confirmed ChildStall + typed survivor record within a bounded time (deterministic injected time + fake progress/alive, mirroring test_freeze_detect.rs).
PT-106-B: no-false-positive GREEN — a progressing child never flags; a cleanly-exited child is deregistered, no ChildStall; double-signal (alive+no-progress) + debounce + recovery GREEN.

PT-106-C: `cargo test -p handshake-native no_silent_hang_end_to_end` GREEN (HARD-BOUNDED) — the CAPSTONE: a hung in-app operation -> StalledOperation (MT-105) AND a hung child -> ChildStall (Palmistry), BOTH within a bounded time, on the real binaries; the hours-long silent hang proven impossible.

PT-106-D: typed-allowlist scan (no free text on the child-stall path) + the all-tests-hard-bounded source review (#[ignore]-with-reason any headless IPC deadlocker, MT-092 precedent) + panel Tier-3 ChildStall surface; both crates + diag-ring build clean + clippy -D warnings + full suite -j 2 green; external target dirs; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty.
implementation_notes: GENERALIZE MT-091, do not fork it sloppily: the ChildStallDetector is the FreezeDetector pattern applied per-child — track each child's last-progress value + the Palmistry monotonic Instant it last advanced; staleness = now - last_advance; reset-on-advance; threshold gate; the double-signal is (stale progress) AND (the child process handle has NOT signaled exit = provably alive but not progressing). A headless child has no message pump to SendMessageTimeout-probe, so 'process-alive + no-progress' IS the corroboration (the analogue of MT-091's hung-window probe for a GUI). Keep the named-const cadence/threshold discipline + the compile-time relationship asserts MT-091 uses.

REUSE the MT-089 process-handle wait for the child's alive/exited signal: the ParentWatch/WindowsParentWatch OpenProcess+WaitForSingleObject+GetExitCodeProcess pattern generalizes to a child handle (a ChildWatch). A child that EXITS is deregistered (a finished child is not a stalled child — AC-106-2); a child that is ALIVE but not progressing is the stall.

LIVENESS SOURCE per child (two supported kinds, typed): (1) the child publishes its OWN ring/heartbeat slot (the MT-084 idea per-child) read via the MT-090 zero-cooperation reader; OR (2) the child touches/updates a per-child LIVENESS FILE (an mtime/counter Palmistry polls) — the field-standard touch-file/keepalive technique for a child that does not share the ring. The control message says WHICH kind (a typed liveness_kind enum) + the source reference (a ring name or a file path reference — a path reference, never project content).

CONTROL MESSAGE: add ControlMessage::RegisterChild { pid: u32, liveness_kind, liveness_ref, threshold_ms } + DeregisterChild { pid } to control.rs, reusing the existing newline-delimited-JSON ControlMessage framing (Shutdown/Ping/...). Typed integers + a liveness reference only. The control socket stays CONTROL-only (§6.13.4): liveness is observed PASSIVELY (ring read / file poll), never requested over the socket — a hung child could not answer (the same reason MT-089/090 forbid socket-based liveness).

POLL THREAD: mirror spawn_freeze_poll exactly — a dedicated child-stall poll thread (NOT the control loop), the MT-091 ~200-500ms cadence band, reset-on-advance, the debounced healthy->stalled edge (record once), recovery on resume. Add it to the run_watcher teardown joins alongside the freeze + watch handles. It must NEVER block on a child (only observe the child's progress source + alive-state — the MT-081 wait-free principle, per-child).

TYPED SURVIVOR RECORD: reuse the MT-093 survivor-store / MT-089 build+persist_survivor_record discipline for a ChildStall record (kind=ChildStall, child pid, session id, stale_ms, last progress counter/ts, typed reason code, optional liveness-file path REFERENCE). Persist next to the ring like the MT-089 survivor JSON; surface in the §10.12.5 Tier-3 panel section (MT-087/093). NO free text.

THE CAPSTONE (AC-106-CAPSTONE) is the deliverable the operator demanded: ONE hard-bounded end-to-end test, REAL binaries, that forces a hung in-app op AND a hung child and asserts StalledOperation (MT-105) AND ChildStall (Palmistry) BOTH in seconds. The hung child should be a tiny TEST-ONLY helper (a sleeps-forever-after-registering binary, or a documented palmistry test seam) so it is deterministic + feature-gated, not a shipped behavior. Locate the built palmistry.exe via the external target dir / current_exe()-relative resolution (the MT-094 path).

BOUNDED-TEST RULE (AC-106-6, MANDATORY — the irony of the 2026-06-28 incident being a TEST hang is the point): every spawn/wait uses an explicit timeout (wait_for_exit/connect/read with a hard deadline); #[ignore]-with-a-typed-reason any IPC test that deadlocks headless (run --ignored on a real host), exactly the MT-092 precedent. A stall-detection test that itself hangs unbounded would reproduce the very incident — do not let it.

HONESTY (Spec-Realism, no-mock): the child Palmistry watches must be a REAL spawned process with a REAL progress source; do NOT fabricate a watched child Handshake does not spawn. Where MT-100/101's native spawn path is a typed blocker today, register the children Handshake genuinely spawns + record an honest typed note for the not-yet-reachable paths. Where a managed backend is needed for a live half, gate it NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF, never fake it.

Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the no-false-positive gate (AC-106-2) + the bounded-time capstone (AC-106-CAPSTONE) + the all-tests-hard-bounded rule (AC-106-6) are the gates — a per-child detector that cries wolf, or a capstone that does not catch BOTH a hung op and a hung child in seconds, or a test that itself hangs, each fails the MT.
ports_from_react: NONE (native child-process stall watch + no-silent-hang capstone)
binds_backend_api: NONE. Palmistry watches the children via their process handle (alive/exited, the MT-089 OpenProcess pattern) + a passive progress source (a per-child ring read via MT-090, or a liveness-file poll). It never calls src/backend. The Handshake-side child registration is a client-side control-socket message only. FR kept as-is. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only; no SQLite.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: A ChildStall produces a Tier 3 typed survivor record persisted locally + surfaced in the Tier-3 panel section; forwarding it into the Tier 1 FR at recovery follows the MT-093 survivor-forward family (the existing runtime_chat_event route, reuse-via-API), not this MT. FR kept as-is/forbidden.

The capstone (AC-106-CAPSTONE) asserts the Tier 2 internal_diagnostics StalledOperation (MT-105) fires for the hung in-app operation alongside the Palmistry ChildStall — the cross-tier link (Tier 2 in-app stall + Tier 3 child stall) is exercised end-to-end here. Palmistry also reads the MT-081 ring (where MT-105 writes StalledOperation) via the MT-090 zero-cooperation reader.

This MT IS the Tier 3 child-process stall watch: Palmistry expands from watching only Handshake to watching the spawned children (pid handle alive/exited + a passive per-child progress source), detecting an alive-but-stalled child the MT-091 double-signal way with the no-false-positive gate, and surfacing a typed ChildStall survivor record. The end-to-end no-silent-hang capstone is proven on the real spawned palmistry.exe + the real HandshakeApp. Built + proven here.

handoff.coder_session: KERNEL_BUILDER-20260630-061317 product_commit=547b29c9; CODEX-20260701 state-recovery proof refresh: built real palmistry.exe with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target, then cargo test -j 1 --manifest-path src/frontend/palmistry/Cargo.toml --test test_child_stall -- --nocapture => 6/6 passed; then HANDSHAKE_PALMISTRY_EXE=../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target/debug/palmistry.exe cargo test -j 1 --manifest-path src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml --test test_no_silent_hang_end_to_end -- --include-ignored --nocapture => ignored real-binary capstone 1/1 passed in 2.55s on 2026-07-01. Post-review subagents Raman/Kuhn found no MT-106 product blockers after capstone proof; residual clean-shutdown proof is outside the no-silent-hang contract.
handoff.depends_on_note: Requires MT-105's DiagEventCode::StalledOperation + the in-app OperationWatchdog (the capstone asserts a StalledOperation fires for the hung operation). MT-105 must land first; this MT reuses its typed code via the MT-090 reader, it does not re-add it.

risks: RISK-106-1: false positive — a child that is slow-but-progressing, or one that exited cleanly, is flagged as stalled (the MT-091 RISK-011-1 class, per-child). Mitigate: reset-on-advance against a monotonic Instant + the double-signal gate (stale progress AND process-alive); a cleanly-exited child is deregistered, not stalled (AC-106-2/3).

RISK-106-2: false negative — a real child stall is MISSED (wrong staleness reference / wall-clock jump / never resetting last-progress, or the watcher blocking on the stalled child). Mitigate: monotonic reference (AC-106-8) + the watcher only OBSERVES the child's progress source + alive-state, never waits on the child (AC-106-1).

RISK-106-3: liveness requested over the control socket (a blocking request-response a hung child can never answer — the MT-089 RISK-009-3 / §6.13.4 class). Mitigate: the control socket is CONTROL-only (register/deregister); per-child liveness is observed PASSIVELY (ring read / file poll), documented.

RISK-106-4: free text (a child command / args / path) smuggled into the ChildStall record or a control message, breaking the typed allowlist. Mitigate: typed integers + a liveness-source path REFERENCE only; AC-106-5 source+value scan (the MT-093 typed-allowlist discipline).

RISK-106-5 (THE IRONIC FAILURE): a stall-detection test itself deadlocks unbounded and wastes hours — reproducing the exact 2026-06-28 incident. Mitigate: AC-106-6 — every spawn/wait is hard-bounded (wait_for_exit/connect/read with an explicit timeout); #[ignore]-with-reason any headless IPC deadlocker (the MT-092 precedent).

RISK-106-6: a fabricated watched child (Handshake does not actually spawn it) makes the capstone a mock, not a real proof. Mitigate: the capstone uses a REAL spawned hung child + the real binaries; honest typed notes where MT-100/101 native spawn paths are blockers (no fabricated child).

RISK-106-7: the child-stall poll thread blocks the control loop / the freeze poll (the MT-091 RISK-011-4 class). Mitigate: its OWN dedicated thread (mirror spawn_freeze_poll), bounded observation, added to the teardown joins.
RISK-106-8: a tracked PNG / repo-local target leaks. Mitigate: external target dirs; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty (AC-106-9).
minimum_controls: MC-106-1: alive-but-stalled child -> confirmed ChildStall + typed record within a bounded time; no false positive on a progressing/cleanly-exited child (AC-106-1/2).
MC-106-2: double-signal (alive+no-progress) + debounce + recovery + monotonic reference (AC-106-3/8).
MC-106-3: THE CAPSTONE — a hung in-app op (StalledOperation, MT-105) AND a hung child (ChildStall, Palmistry) both caught in seconds on the real binaries (AC-106-CAPSTONE).

MC-106-4: ALL tests hard-bounded (#[ignore]-with-reason headless IPC deadlockers, MT-092 precedent) + typed-allowlist + Tier-3 panel surface + external target dirs + clippy -D warnings + full suite -j 2 green (AC-106-5/6/7/9).

research_provenance.source: WP-KERNEL-012 field research (June 2026) + the 2026-06-28 silent-subprocess-deadlock incident; generalizes the MT-091 freeze-detection precedent (double-signal, no-false-positive, monotonic reference) to per-child liveness, reuses the MT-089 process-handle wait + MT-093 survivor-record discipline, and consumes the MT-105 in-app StalledOperation for the capstone
research_provenance.field_stack: Process supervision + per-child liveness watchdog (the Crashpad/watchdog external-observer model extended to the SUPERVISED CHILDREN): a parent's external watcher holds each child's OS process handle (alive vs exited) AND observes a per-child progress signal (a shared heartbeat slot, the MT-084 idea per-child, or a touch-file/keepalive an mtime the watcher polls — the field-standard liveness-file technique for a process that does not share memory). An alive-but-no-progress child is the stall (the analogue of MT-091's stale-heartbeat-plus-hung-window double signal: process-alive is the corroboration for a headless child with no message pump). The bounded-time capstone proves both the in-app-operation tier (MT-105) and the child tier (this MT) catch a hang in seconds — the systemd-watchdog / embedded-WDT 'kick or you are presumed dead' guarantee, applied to Handshake's spawned children.

crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.windows-sys: 0.61 (match handshake-native / MT-089; Win32_Foundation + Win32_System_Threading for the child OpenProcess/WaitForSingleObject/GetExitCodeProcess handle, reused from MT-089)
crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.handshake-diag-ring: path dep (MT-081; the StalledOperation code is MT-105's; read via the MT-090 reader)
crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.serde_json: 1 (typed ChildStall survivor record + the RegisterChild control message; palmistry already has it)
crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.std-only-detector: the ChildStallDetector is std-only (std::time::Instant monotonic + std::thread poll), mirroring the MT-091 FreezeDetector — no new external crate for the detection math

research_provenance.motivating_incident: 2026-06-28: a spawned subprocess (a cargo test doing minidumper IPC — the MT-092 known hanger) DEADLOCKED SILENTLY for HOURS with no detection and no observer. Palmistry watched only Handshake, not the children Handshake spawns, so a deadlocked spawned session/tool/model/test had no watcher. This MT makes the spawned-CHILD half of that class impossible (Palmistry watches the children the SAME double-signal way it watches Handshake) AND delivers the operator-required capstone: a hung in-app operation (MT-105 StalledOperation) AND a hung child (Palmistry ChildStall) are BOTH caught within a BOUNDED time (seconds, not hours) on the real binaries — proving the hours-long silent hang can NEVER happen inside Handshake.
research_provenance.validation_plan: per-child detector (alive-but-stalled -> ChildStall + typed record in bounded time; no false positive on a progressing/exited child; double-signal + debounce + recovery; monotonic reference) + child registration over the control socket + typed-allowlist scan + THE CAPSTONE (hung op + hung child both caught in seconds on real binaries) + ALL tests hard-bounded (#[ignore]-with-reason headless IPC deadlockers, MT-092 precedent) + Tier-3 panel surface; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2; external target dirs.
